title: Build PSP (Podman Socket Proxy): policy-gated Testcontainers runtime for sandboxed agents
description:
Objective
Create PSP as a standalone project that enables Testcontainers usage from sandboxed agents without exposing raw host Podman socket access.

Scope
- Docker-compatible broker API fronting rootless Podman
- Policy enforcement and deny-by-default posture
- Session isolation, cleanup, and auditability
- Clear integration contract for AGS

Success Criteria
- Agents can run integration tests through PSP endpoint
- Dangerous container operations are blocked by policy
- Lifecycle/cleanup is deterministic
- AGS integration requires only minimal wiring

Out of Scope
- Multi-tenant auth system
- Managed cloud service deployment
- Non-Linux runtime parity beyond explicit docs

title: Write ADR + threat model for PSP trust boundaries
description:
Goal
Lock architecture and threat model before implementation.

Deliverables
- ADR describing boundaries: sandbox process, PSP, host Podman API
- Abuse case matrix and mitigations
- Rationale for broker vs raw socket mounting

Acceptance Criteria
- ADR approved and actionable for implementation tasks
- Residual risks explicitly documented
close_reason: ADR and threat model documented in docs/adr/0001-psp-architecture.md

title: Implement PSP MVP Docker-compatible API broker
description:
Goal
Implement core Docker-compatible API broker to satisfy Testcontainers base lifecycle operations.

Scope
- Unix socket listener
- Forward/translate required endpoints to rootless Podman API
- Consistent error mapping for client compatibility

Acceptance Criteria
- End-to-end create/start/inspect/logs/wait/remove works through PSP
- Unsupported endpoints fail with explicit structured errors
close_reason: Implemented Unix-socket PSP broker with endpoint allowlist, backend forwarding, and end-to-end lifecycle tests

title: Add PSP policy engine (deny by default)
description:
Goal
Prevent privilege escalation and unsafe runtime operations.

Policy v1
- deny privileged mode and host namespaces
- deny unrestricted bind mounts (allowlist only)
- deny device mounts and dangerous capability adds
- optional image allow/deny controls

Acceptance Criteria
- Policies are schema-validated and version controlled
- Denials include stable rule IDs and human-readable reasons
- Unit tests cover allow/deny matrix
close_reason: Added schema-validated deny-by-default policy engine with stable rule IDs and allow/deny tests

title: Define Testcontainers compatibility profile and endpoint contract
description:
Goal
Define and document exactly which Docker API features PSP supports for Testcontainers.

Scope
- Identify API calls used by Rust testcontainers flows
- Build compatibility matrix (required, optional, unsupported)
- Document behavior differences from Docker Engine

Acceptance Criteria
- Compatibility profile published in docs
- Contract tests prove required endpoints work
close_reason: Published PSP Testcontainers compatibility profile and added endpoint contract coverage

title: Implement host/port resolution strategy for in-container test clients
description:
Goal
Ensure tests running in sandbox can reliably reach published service ports.

Scope
- Resolve host mapping strategy for Podman-backed runtime
- Validate random host ports and parallel test execution
- Provide deterministic defaults and override knobs

Acceptance Criteria
- Reproducible test shows reliable connectivity from sandbox test process
- Troubleshooting guidance for hostname/port failures exists

title: Implement PSP session lifecycle and orphan cleanup
description:
Goal
Track and clean all resources created via PSP across normal and abnormal exits.

Scope
- Session labeling for containers/networks/volumes
- Cleanup on normal stop and crash-recovery sweep
- Optional keep-on-failure debug mode

Acceptance Criteria
- Repeated runs do not leak resources
- Crash recovery verified by integration test

title: Add structured audit logs and deny diagnostics
description:
Goal
Make policy decisions observable and debuggable.

Scope
- Structured allow/deny logs with rule IDs
- Context fields: session, operation, target image/resource
- Secret-safe logging/redaction

Acceptance Criteria
- Operators can trace blocked requests to specific policy rules
- Logs are parseable and documented

title: Create PSP integration and compatibility test suite
description:
Goal
Validate PSP behavior against real container lifecycle and testcontainers-like flows.

Scope
- Happy path and blocked path tests
- Parallel-run stress tests
- Resource leak assertions

Acceptance Criteria
- CI/local target runs deterministic test suite
- Failures provide clear diagnostics

title: Publish PSP docs: architecture, policy, operations, AGS integration contract
description:
Goal
Document PSP for users and integrators.

Scope
- Architecture and threat model
- Policy file reference and examples
- Operational runbook and troubleshooting
- AGS integration contract/versioning notes

Acceptance Criteria
- New contributor can run and validate PSP from docs alone
